title: Protocol Inheritance 🧬
description: How one rulebook can inherit from another.

slides.title: Rulebooks Inside Rulebooks 📚

quizData.question: If `protocol Combat: Moveable` exists, and a `Player` struct adopts `Combat`... what must the Player implement?
quizData.explanation: Because `Combat` inherits `Moveable`, adopting Combat forces the Struct to fulfill the requirements of both layered protocols.

slides.title: Multiple Adoption 🤝🤝

quizData.question: In the Swift language, can a Class inherit from TWO Superclasses at the same time?

options.text: No. Multiple Inheritance is strictly forbidden in Swift.

quizData.explanation: Swift avoids the dreaded 'Diamond Problem' found in C++. A class gets exactly ONE parent. However, it can adopt Infinite Protocols!
